Meta.defaultDescription: Direction before action. Strategy that aligns marketing, sales and digital.

hero.claim: ORYEN develops a strategy that aligns everything — and helps you choose the right tools and follow-up to guard that line.
hero.sub: No execution without strategy. First get right what must fit.

diagnosis.p1: Marketing attracts leads that sales does not follow up on. Sales hears what lives in the market, but that feedback does not return to marketing. Digital tools are rolled out, but no one checks whether they actually improve anything.
diagnosis.focus: If marketing, sales and digital are not aligned, more execution only makes the problem larger.

approach.headline: First work out the strategy
approach.note1: ORYEN does not start with campaigns, tools or execution. We first develop a strategy that aligns marketing, sales and digital, clarifies what must happen first, and keeps it that way.

steps.name: Map the situation
steps.q: What is there today? What works? What is stuck? Where is alignment missing between marketing, sales and digital?

steps.name: Align
steps.q: Where do marketing, sales and digital clash today? What must connect better so they reinforce instead of block each other?

steps.name: Develop strategy
steps.q: Which choices are really needed now? What first? What is too early? Which approach fits your market, team and commercial reality?

steps.name: Embed
steps.q: Only when direction is right does execution make sense. Then we help set up the right tools, metrics and follow-up so the strategy stays visible and guarded.

featured.title:
Not more visibility.
First clarity on position.
featured.line1: Everyone saw a communications question. More content, more reach.
featured.line2: ORYEN saw a positioning question. Who are we, for whom, and what must never be lost?
featured.line3: Not more communication — sharper choices. Less noise, more coherence.

minis.subtitle: Not another idea. First one right choice.
minis.body: There were enough ideas. Every agency brought another solution. ORYEN first returned to the essence: which problem really needs solving here?
minis.result: One right choice made every other choice simpler.

minis.subtitle: Not roll out at once. First align internally.
minis.body: An international organisation with different units and one shared ambition. ORYEN first brought focus to what must be shared, what must be chosen first, and how the line could stay guarded.

minis.subtitle: Not sound like everyone. First choose what distinguishes you.
minis.body: The reflex was to communicate like every other dealer network. ORYEN saw the real strength was in local identity.
minis.result: What seemed a nuance at first became the strategy.

selection.headline: For those who want marketing, sales and digital
forItems: For companies that feel a lot is happening but the whole does not fit.
For companies that do not want to invest again in something that comes too early.
For companies that want a strategy first that aligns marketing, sales and digital — and clarifies what must happen first and how it stays followed up.
notFor: Want loose campaigns without commercial coherence
Believe in quick promises more than sound analysis
Seek speed above all without first setting the right order

about.headline: ORYEN develops a strategy that aligns marketing, sales and digital
about.headlineEm: — and helps ensure it stays that way.
about.statement: We do not start with doing. We start with seeing what is off — and work out a strategy that realigns marketing, sales and digital.
about.creds: 23 years B2B — HubSpot Gold Partner — Cambridge certified.
about.quote: The most expensive decision is often not the wrong one. It is the one taken too early.

offer.name: Reality Check
offer.body: A Reality Check is not a casual intake or a sales pitch. It is a first strategic exercise where we clarify what is not aligned today — and what is needed first for marketing, sales and digital to work better together.
offer.secondaryBody: No casual intake. No standard sales call. A first clear conversation about what does not connect in your organisation — and what is needed first.
offer.secondaryNote: Personal reply from Christophe. No ticket system.

meta.title: Reality Check — ORYEN
meta.description: The Reality Check is the first strategic step for companies that want marketing, sales and digital aligned — before choosing, building or investing again.

hero.headlineLine2Em: Start with a strategy that fits.
hero.sub: A defined first conversation for companies that sense marketing, sales and digital are not aligned — and want clarity on what must fit before choosing, building or investing again.

offerFrame.label: Reality Check
pillars: For organisations where marketing, sales and digital are not on one line

offerClarity.leftBody: Not a casual intake or a sales pitch. It is a first strategic exercise where ORYEN clarifies what is not aligned today — and what is needed first for marketing, sales and digital to work better together.
offerClarity.rightTitle: For whom — and not for whom
offerClarity.rightLead: For companies that already move, invest and decide — but notice marketing, sales and digital do not work as one.
offerClarity.forBody: You are in the right place if you want clarity first on what must fit, what must happen first, and how that line stays guarded — before you invest again in execution, tools or programmes.
welItems: Leaders who sense a lot is happening but the whole does not fit
Teams that do not want to invest again in something that comes too early
Organisations that want a strategic base before they execute
notForItems: Want loose campaigns without commercial coherence
Seek speed above all without first setting the right order

whatYouGet.headline: What you take away
whatYouGet.subline: A sharp first strategic exercise — not a report for the shelf.
items: What really sits behind the question on the table
What must be decided first now
What is too early and better waits
Which tools, agreements and metrics are needed to guard alignment

steps.title: We map what is there today.
steps.body: What works? What is stuck? Where is alignment missing between marketing, sales and digital?

steps.title: We define what must fit first.
steps.body: Which choices are needed now? What is too early? Where is the real confusion?

steps.title: We clarify the right next step.
steps.body: Which strategy restores the line? Which tools and follow-up help guard that line?

pricing.name: Reality Check
pricing.body: No trajectory without direction. The Reality Check first brings clarity on what must fit, what must happen first, and which choices you are better not making yet.
includedItems: A first strategic exploration, tailored to your situation
Clarity on what must fit first — and what is still too early
Direction for next steps — no mandatory follow-on programme

reassurance.headline: Not a casual intake. A first strategic conversation.
reassurance.body: We do not only look at what you ask. We look at what sits beneath that question — and what must be aligned first so further execution makes sense.
reassurance.note: Personal reply from Christophe. No ticket system.

closing.line1: The most expensive decision is often not the wrong one.
closing.line2: It is the one taken too early.

aanpak.title: The ORYEN method.
aanpak.intro: We first develop a strategy that aligns marketing, sales and digital. From there we make the right choices in tools, agreements and follow-up.
